agent_outputs.raw: The pilot cannot support the network-wide expansion recommendation. Three problems: First, pilot merchants were large retailers with dedicated risk teams who self-selected — they are systematically unlike smaller or less sophisticated merchants in the network. Second, some pilot merchants also adopted stronger checkout authentication during the same period, creating a competing explanation for reduced fraud losses. Third, fraud labels are observable only for approved transactions; if pilot merchants had different approval rates than non-pilot merchants, the fraud rate comparison is not controlled for this selection effect. Same-calendar-week matching controls for timing but not for these structural differences. A randomized test with matched merchants and stable authentication settings is required.

The pilot design cannot support a causal inference about model performance. Three issues. Self-selection of large sophisticated merchants creates a different baseline population from the rest of the network. Concurrent authentication improvements in some pilot merchants provide an alternative explanation for fraud reduction. Approved-only label observability means that changes in approval rates across merchant types affect what fraud is even measurable. The same-calendar-week comparison controls for one dimension (time) but leaves three others uncontrolled. Critique wins.

The pilot result is promising but cannot justify network-wide expansion. The causal inference is blocked by three confounds: merchant self-selection (large retailers with risk teams), concurrent authentication changes in pilot merchants, and differential label observability between merchants with different approval policies. A properly controlled within-merchant experiment or a randomized switchback design with stable authentication is needed before network expansion. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. All three agents identify the same three confounds. Same-calendar-week comparison is correctly exonerated as a timing control that does not address structural confounds.

etd_constraint.condition: Randomized or switchback test within comparable merchants holding authentication settings constant; track approval rates and fraud outcomes consistently
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Within-merchant randomized result shows fraud improvement shrinks after controlling for authentication and approval-rate differences between merchant types
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Within comparable merchants with stable authentication, model shows significant fraud reduction and fewer false declines
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: Fraud improves on some merchants but effect size varies with authentication adoption and approval rate shifts
